name: healthcare_communications_outage_marrowbridge_auto_ben
premise: A Healthcare clinical command center is in a fast-moving incident involving healthcare communications outage marrowbridge auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next shift change. Every delay increases patient safety, triage integrity, and privacy. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Marco Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Marco Kowalski, accountable for a defensible call in Healthcare when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Amina Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Amina Ishikawa, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Healthcare.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next shift change. Evidence is partial. Marco Kowalski must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Marco Kowalski do?
